Controls.Title: Multi-Level Scenes
Controls.Build: Build a multi-level scene from a folder

StairsConfig.Title: Configure stairs zone
StairsConfig.LevelHint: The stairs zone only triggers for tokens on this floor. "Every level" makes it a single stairwell working on every floor.
StairsConfig.DirectionHint: Where tokens may go from this zone: up only, down only, or both directions.

Stairs.Title: Stairs
Stairs.Prompt: {name} is standing on the stairs. Where to?

Build.Title: Build multi-level scene
Build.Hint: Pick the folder containing the floor scenes. Each scene name must end with a number: 00 = basement, 01 = ground floor, the highest number is the roof.
Build.SkippedNoNumber: Scene "{name}" skipped: its name does not end with a number.
Build.NeedTwo: At least 2 scenes with a trailing number are required.
Build.DuplicateLevel: Two scenes share the same floor number ({level}).
Build.DimMismatch: Warning: the scenes do not all share the same dimensions. The lowest floor's dimensions will be used.
Build.NoBackground: Scene "{name}" has no background image: no tile was created for that floor.
Build.Done: Scene "{name}" created with {count} floors. Now draw the stairs and building zones with the module tools.

FloorHeight.Name: Floor height
FloorHeight.Hint: Elevation difference (in grid distance units, e.g. feet or meters) between one floor and the next.

DefaultLevel.Name: Default starting level
DefaultLevel.Hint: Level assigned to newly placed tokens (usually 1 = ground floor, since 00 is the basement).

ShowStairsToPlayers.Name: Show stairs to players
ShowStairsToPlayers.Hint: If enabled, players see a faint orange outline over stairs zones.

Info.FloorHeightFixed: Multi-Level Scenes: floor height corrected from 4.5 to 5.

Warn.NotComposite: This is not a multi-level scene. Create one with the "Build" tool.
Warn.NoSuchLevel: Level {level} does not exist in this scene.
